summary: Comprehensive testing of Staticify WordPress to Static Deployer. Fixed critical bug in /api/history endpoint. All 30 backend tests pass. Frontend navigation, CRUD UI, and schedules working correctly.

fixed.issue: Returned 500 due to legacy data with old schema (profile_id/profile_name instead of deployment_config_id/deployment_name)
fixed.fix: Added normalize_history_item() function to handle legacy data with default values for missing fields

design_issues.issue: Legacy deployment entries show 'Legacy Source' and 'Legacy Destination' placeholders instead of actual names
design_issues.impact: LOW - Only affects old data created before schema change

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all pages load, navigation works, CRUD operations functional)

seed_data_creation: Test data created and cleaned up during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: The app uses a Source → Destination → Deployments model. Schedules use interval_hours (integer) instead of cron expressions. Legacy data in deployment_history collection had old schema with profile_id/profile_name - this was fixed by adding normalize_history_item() function.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Pydantic response validation failed because legacy data in MongoDB had fields (profile_id, profile_name) from previous schema iteration, but the new DeploymentHistory model required different fields (deployment_config_id, deployment_name, source_name, destination_name)
rca_of_the_issue.error_message: ResponseValidationError: 4 validation errors: deployment_config_id missing, deployment_name missing, source_name missing, destination_name missing
rca_of_the_issue.solution: Added normalize_history_item() function that maps legacy fields to new schema and provides defaults for missing required fields
rca_of_the_issue.prevention: When refactoring database schemas, consider adding migration scripts or compatibility layers for existing data

tests_passed: Deployment Configs CRUD with source/destination linking
